title: Characterization Process (Simplified Input)
description: A guided form to describe a characterization experiment with full provenance context. Three fields are mandatory: who performed the experiment (operator), which device was used, and which specimen was characterised. This input will be automatically converted to a standardised RDF representation (OBI/PMDCo OO-LD).

process_name.title: Process Name
process_name.description: A human-readable name for this characterization experiment. Example: 'Tensile test 316L batch 1 — QA run'.

process_id.title: Process ID
process_id.description: Optional. Identifier (IRI slug) for this process node in the RDF graph. Auto-derived from process_name if omitted.

description.title: Description
description.description: Optional free-text description of this experiment.

operator_iri.title: Operator (Expert) IRI
operator_iri.description: IRI of the person (expert or technician) who conducted this experiment. Must reference an existing node in the knowledge graph.

device_iri.title: Measurement Device IRI
device_iri.description: IRI of the measurement device used in this experiment. Must reference a node registered with the measurement-device/PMDCo schema.

specimen_iri.title: Specimen IRI
specimen_iri.description: IRI of the specimen being characterised. Must reference a node registered with the specimen/PMDCo schema.

date.title: Experiment Date
date.description: Date and time when the experiment was conducted, in ISO 8601 format (e.g. '2024-11-15T09:30:00').

step_reference.title: Step Result Reference
step_reference.description: IRI of the detailed characterization step result in the knowledge graph (e.g. a tto:TensileTest node). Add this after the detailed result has been recorded.

preceded_by.title: Preceded By
preceded_by.description: IRIs of characterization processes that directly precede this one.

conditions.title: Test Conditions
conditions.description: Quantitative conditions under which this experiment was carried out (e.g. strain rate, temperature, test standard).
